#set heading(numbering: "1.")
#set math.equation(numbering: "(1)")

#align(center, [
  #text(size: 1.5em, weight: "bold")[Extended Inflation from Strings]
  #v(1em)
  Source-backed arXiv sample
  #v(1em)
])

= Extracted Lists

The list environments below are extracted from arXiv LaTeX source and wrapped for pdfLaTeX validation.

/ Fig.1: Plot of the region in parameter space (a, b) allowed by the inflationary conditions in the text. The solid lines represent those bounds which do not depend on the scale M of the phase transition. The dashed curve correspond to the bounds associated with the scale M = 10^11 GeV and the dotted curve to those related to M = 10^16 GeV. The allowed region is the one below the curves. The border b = 0 is excluded from it. We have labelled the curves as follows: QG corresponds to the condition associated to neglecting quantum gravity effects, #text("$\\Phi$") corresponds to the condition for an increasing scalar field, RW corresponds to the bound on isotropy and homogeneity at the time of nucleosynthesis and pN--NS corresponds to the bounds from post--Newtonian experiments and the nucleosynthesis bound on #text("$\\omega$").
